title: DistilBERT, a distilled version of BERT: smaller, faster, cheaper and lighter
objective: To propose a method for pre-training a smaller, general-purpose language representation model, called DistilBERT, which can be fine-tuned with good performance on various tasks, addressing the challenge of operating large pre-trained models under constrained computational budgets in NLP.

methodology.approach: Leveraged knowledge distillation during the pre-training phase to create a smaller version of a BERT model, contrasting with prior work that primarily used distillation for task-specific models.

key_findings.evidence: reduce the size of a BERT model by 40%

key_findings.finding: High retention of original model's capabilities.

conclusion: The work successfully introduces DistilBERT, demonstrating that applying knowledge distillation during the pre-training phase can drastically reduce the size of a BERT model while largely preserving its performance across various language tasks, making large models more viable for constrained environments.
limitations: Not explicitly mentioned in the provided text.
